changes.result: CRITICAL FINDING — STOP GATE 1 PRE-CONFIRMED FOR ALL 4
changes.finding: CONFIRMED from direct file reads. (1) Staged PSI pkg_kb52c_rbc_high_erythrocytosis_pattern: internal package_id at line 3 = pkg_kb52c_rbc_high_erythrocytosis_pattern. Production package pkg_kb58_rbc_high_erythrocytosis_pattern exists (package_manifest.yaml:1 confirms package_id: pkg_kb58_rbc_high_erythrocytosis_pattern, source: cbc_hematology_pass_3.json, author: KB-S58). No promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml present. (2-4) Same pattern confirmed for rbc_low_iron_restricted_anemia_pattern, rdw_cv_high_iron_deficiency_anisocytosis, rdw_cv_high_mixed_red_cell_population_pattern from P1-16 manifest. No pkg_kb52c_rbc_* or pkg_kb52c_rdw_cv_* production packages exist in knowledge_bus/packages/ (Glob returns empty). No identity-normalisation tooling in repository (P1-16_psi_identity_blocker_remediation.md explicit). Cross-ID placement rejected GPT Option B in P1-15. STOP gate 1 fires for all 4. All 4 must be classified BLOCKED_PACKAGE_IDENTITY_UNRESOLVED or BLOCKED_REQUIRES_SOURCE_RESEARCH_ADJUDICATION.

changes.result: CRITICAL FINDING — SSOT PRESENT BUT VALIDATOR HARDCODES AS DERIVED
changes.finding: CONFIRMED: transferrin_saturation IS present in backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml at line 1865 as a canonical top-level key (unit: '%', category: Iron Studies, data_type: numeric, system: nutritional). It is NOT missing from the SSOT. HOWEVER: backend/scripts/validate_staged_psi_activation_readiness.py line 32 hardcodes DERIVED_MARKER_IDS = frozenset({'transferrin_saturation'}). At line 261, the validator checks if sm_id in DERIVED_MARKER_IDS and appends 'derived-marker supporting dependency: transferrin_saturation' blocker regardless of SSOT presence. This means all 7 derived-marker candidates will remain blocked by the validator even though transferrin_saturation is a valid SSOT key. The only resolution is to remove transferrin_saturation from DERIVED_MARKER_IDS in backend/scripts/validate_staged_psi_activation_readiness.py — this is a backend script edit, which is out of Cursor's agent boundary (automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:49). STOP gate 2 fires for all 7 derived-marker candidates. All 7 must be classified BLOCKED_REQUIRES_CORE_BACKEND_AGENT.

All 7 BLOCKED_DERIVED_MARKER_DEPENDENCY candidates confirmed from P1-14_activation_readiness_cohort_manifest.yaml lines 211-269: (1) pkg_kb52c_ferritin_high_inflammatory_hyperferritinemia (batch_b), (2) pkg_kb52c_ferritin_high_iron_overload_context (batch_b), (3) pkg_kb61_transferrin_high_iron_deficiency_transport_upregulation (batch_b), (4) pkg_kb52c_iron_high_hepatocellular_or_hemolytic_release (batch_c — also BLOCKED_FRAME_AUTHORITY + BLOCKED_MEDICAL_REVIEW_REQUIRED), (5) pkg_kb52c_iron_high_iron_overload_context (batch_c — also BLOCKED_FRAME_AUTHORITY + BLOCKED_MEDICAL_REVIEW_REQUIRED), (6) pkg_kb52c_iron_low_absolute_iron_deficiency (batch_c — also BLOCKED_FRAME_AUTHORITY + BLOCKED_MEDICAL_REVIEW_REQUIRED), (7) pkg_kb52c_iron_low_functional_iron_restriction_inflammation (batch_c — also BLOCKED_FRAME_AUTHORITY + BLOCKED_MEDICAL_REVIEW_REQUIRED). Grep confirms transferrin_saturation as supporting_markers biomarker_id in staged PSI for candidates 1-3 and 4-7. Note: candidates 4-7 carry additional medical-review and frame-authority blockers independent of the derived-marker issue.
changes.citation: docs/sprints/beta_readiness/P1-14_activation_readiness_cohort_manifest.yaml:211-269; knowledge_bus/generated_pilot/p1_11_batch_b/pkg_kb52c_ferritin_high_inflammatory_hyperferritinemia/promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml (transferrin_saturation in supporting_markers confirmed via grep); knowledge_bus/generated_pilot/p1_12_batch_c/pkg_kb52c_iron_high_hepatocellular_or_hemolytic_release/promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml (transferrin_saturation confirmed)

changes.finding: Behavioural surface area is explicitly defined in the prompt. Cursor may only edit: Knowledge Bus package manifests, production PSI artefacts, sprint documentation, beta-readiness deliverable register, blocker/adjudication manifests. Cursor may NOT edit backend/ssot/, backend/core/, backend/scripts/, backend/tests/, frontend/, DTO/report/orchestrator/scoring/Gemini/parser/runtime files. All STOP gates (1-4) explicitly protect against scope creep into backend, medical content alteration, or cross-ID PSI placement. No Intelligence Core module is touched.

Mandatory validation for any newly opted-in candidate per Phase 5: (1) validate_promoted_signal_intelligence.py per PSI, (2) validate_knowledge_package.py per package, (3) validate_staged_psi_activation_readiness.py rerun on full estate. P1-16 baseline: psi_files_found=41, production_opt_ins_found=42, activation_ready_count=4. If no candidates are opted in this sprint (all-STOP outcome), the validator must still be rerun and must not regress.

Risk 1 — Cross-ID PSI placement via pkg_kb58_* packages: The 4 identity candidates have matching pkg_kb58_* production packages that contain no PSI. Cursor may attempt to place a PSI file into pkg_kb58_* packages based on the clinical equivalence of the underlying research. This is explicitly prohibited: the staged PSI internal package_id (pkg_kb52c_*) differs from the host production package_id (pkg_kb58_*), and no deterministic identity-normalisation tooling exists. STOP gate 1 fires. Do NOT do this. Risk 2 — Validator modification to unblock derived-marker candidates: The only path to resolving the 7 transferrin_saturation-blocked candidates is removing transferrin_saturation from DERIVED_MARKER_IDS at validate_staged_psi_activation_readiness.py:32. This is a backend/scripts/ file and is out of scope. Cursor must NOT modify it. Classify all 7 as BLOCKED_REQUIRES_CORE_BACKEND_AGENT and produce the Phase 6 handoff manifest.

Given STOP gate 1 fires for all 4 identity candidates and STOP gate 2 fires for all 7 derived-marker candidates, the sprint will produce report-only output. The prompt explicitly accounts for this: 'This sprint must not become report-only unless all candidates hit STOP gates' (automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:27). The contingency is covered — the 3 mandatory output artefacts (P1-17_blocker_resolution_manifest.yaml, P1-17_core_backend_handoff_manifest.yaml, P1-17_remaining_psi_blocker_resolution.md) and the BUILD_DELIVERABLE_REGISTER.md update remain mandatory deliverables regardless of opt-in count.

Git status at conversation start shows automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md as modified on main (working tree). The sprint branch sprint/P1-17-remaining-psi-blocker-resolution must be confirmed clean before start. SOP §14 prohibits tooling files (.codex/, .cursor/, .vscode/, AGENTS.md) in sprint branches. Prior session note: knowledge-bus-medical-intelligence Cursor rule was added; confirm it does not leak into sprint branch. Before finish: git status --short must show only in-scope files.
changes.citation: gitStatus (conversation context: M automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md); automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:113-122 (tooling leakage prohibition)

changes.finding: Determinism risk 1: If any candidate is incorrectly advanced to READY_FOR_PRODUCTION_OPT_IN without clearing STOP gate 1 or 2, a cross-ID PSI or validator-blocked PSI enters production — this would be an architectural defect. STOP gates prevent this if Cursor applies them correctly. Determinism risk 2: The transferrin_saturation SSOT presence (biomarkers.yaml:1865) may mislead Cursor into believing the 7 derived-marker candidates can be resolved at the Knowledge Bus level. They cannot — the blocker is the hardcoded DERIVED_MARKER_IDS in the validator, not SSOT absence. This hardening note must be understood before Phase 3 adjudication. Determinism risk 3: Candidates 4-7 of the derived-marker cohort carry medical-review and frame-authority blockers independent of the derived-marker issue. Even if the validator DERIVED_MARKER_IDS were fixed by a future backend sprint, these 4 candidates would still require medical review before opt-in. The handoff manifest must capture all blocking layers.

overall_verdict: HARDENED. All 5 front-matter fields confirmed valid. All 12 mandatory authority files confirmed present. Two critical pre-confirmed findings: (1) STOP gate 1 fires for all 4 package-identity candidates — staged PSI internal package_id is pkg_kb52c_*, production packages exist only as pkg_kb58_*, no pkg_kb52c_rbc_*/rdw_cv_* production packages exist, no identity-normalisation tooling in repository, cross-ID placement rejected GPT Option B in P1-15; (2) STOP gate 2 fires for all 7 derived-marker candidates — transferrin_saturation IS in backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml:1865 but is hardcoded in DERIVED_MARKER_IDS frozenset at backend/scripts/validate_staged_psi_activation_readiness.py:32, validator blocker is not an SSOT gap but a backend script constraint, editing the validator is out of agent boundary, all 7 must be classified BLOCKED_REQUIRES_CORE_BACKEND_AGENT. Sprint outcome is expected to be report-only with mandatory Phase 6 handoff manifest and Phase 7 sprint report as deliverables. Two scope-creep risks surfaced and documented.
